name: Folder — Layered Memory Archive
description: A tactile H5 archive of four file folders. Each folder has a physically believable back cover, front cover, and smoothly connected tab, then opens into layered paper, tape, photo, and sticker compositions. Best when a relationship can be organized as chapters: a shared errand, a quiet ritual, a saved quote, a promise, or a small story that deserves to be filed with care.

slots.purpose: Photos, screenshots, chat excerpts, notes, dates, places, nicknames, repeated phrases, and small story fragments that make each folder feel like a real archive from the relationship.
grounding_rules: Every visible folder label, caption, tab, and story note must trace to a concrete detail, quote, habit, place, date, object, or phrase from the user's material.
Use the language and address form the user naturally uses with the recipient in the final gift copy.
Never make the archive feel generic by filling it with broad praise, vague romance, or ungrounded birthday-copy.

slots.purpose: A per-folder editorial plan. Each folder gets one larger chapter theme, one memory anchor, and a few concrete details or quotations that can be turned into tabs, captions, and layered paper notes.

output_fields_per_folder.folder_id: stable runtime id such as row1, row2, row3, row4, or a custom chapter key
output_fields_per_folder.chapter_theme: the larger idea that holds the folder together, e.g. errands as shorthand, shared quiet, saved words, future plans
output_fields_per_folder.memory_anchor: the specific source detail this folder preserves
output_fields_per_folder.quoted_or_echoed_detail: a short quote, nickname, date, food, place, screenshot line, or repeated phrase from the user's material
output_fields_per_folder.visual_motif: what the photo arrangement, papers, tape, and sticker group should form as one readable image
output_fields_per_folder.tab_title: short folder tab title in the user's habitual language
output_fields_per_folder.tab_subtitle: specific subtitle, not a broad mood label

rules: A folder is a chapter with evidence, not a generic scrapbook page.
Write tabs, captions, and notes in the user's usual language with the recipient.
If material is sparse, make fewer folders and let each folder be quieter rather than padding with generic filler.

slots.purpose: The non-negotiable physical drawing rules for the folder shell.
rules: Respect common file-folder proportions: a broad body, a tab connected to the shell, and enough visible front cover to read as a folded object.
Every folder must retain a back cover and a front cover; do not replace the shell with a flat rectangle or isolated tab.
The tab must connect smoothly to the folder body with matching curvature and side-specific placement.
Layered papers, photos, and stickers can peek out, but they should feel held by the folder instead of floating unrelated to it.

runtime_mapping.source: canonical SVG folder-back, folder-front, folder-lip, and open spread elements in template-source/520-folder-gift.html

slots.purpose: Generated or processed photos that fit the folder's chosen layout: vertical three-strip, four-grid, polaroid, framed photo, mini receipt photo, or open spread image.
identity_rules: When recipient photos are available, preserve recognizable face shape, hair, age, skin tone, and styling across all generated photos.
When no photos are available, keep a consistent non-specific protagonist design across folders.
Prefer candid, physical poses that suit paper/photo handling: leaning over a shopping basket, holding a receipt, sharing earphones, sitting near a desk, or being tucked into a polaroid frame.
composition_rules: Use diverse photo formats across the archive: at least two among vertical three-strip, four-grid, polaroid, framed photo, bordered mini photo, and receipt-like photo.
Crop faces and hands safely inside the chosen frame. Do not cover key gestures with tapes or sticker clusters.
Generated photos should leave usable margins for paper shadows, tape overlaps, captions, and tab text.
The open folder should contain a clear hero photo plus supporting smaller photos, not a grid of equal-weight images everywhere.
slots.model_hint: Use an image model that supports identity-preserving multi-image prompts when user photos are provided.

slots.purpose: Use the prepared paper bases, notes, cards, clipboard surfaces, torn scraps, tapes, and textured papers to create depth and writable/photo surfaces. Do not redraw these from scratch unless a needed surface is missing.
slots.selection_hint: Choose base papers and tape/paper scraps that support the folder's chapter theme and photo format: lined paper for a quote, grid paper for a record-like chapter, torn notes for a small confession, tapes to physically hold photo edges.
placement_rules: Papers should sit inside the folder bounds or intentionally peek from the folder mouth.
Layer tapes and paper scraps under or over photos to create believable physical overlap.
Do not scatter many unrelated papers; group them into 1-3 readable paper stacks.

runtime_mapping.layout_source: selector image replacements plus thumbnail/open/closed layout overrides

slots.selection_hint: Pick elements that belong to the chapter's story: clips, labels, frames, charms, devices, study tools, tickets, icons, and small decorative anchors. Element choices should vary across folders rather than repeating the same decorative language.
placement_rules: All stickers should stay inside the folder shell or open spread unless they are intentionally taped to the outside cover.
Cluster stickers into small semantic groups that combine with papers and photos, such as a receipt corner, a photo held by two clips, or a desk-note stack.
Avoid isolated, evenly distributed sticker scatter.
Frames, screens, cards, and other container-like stickers must contain a photo, paper, or text layer.

runtime_mapping.layout_source: selector image replacements plus thumbnail/open/closed layout overrides

slots.purpose: Archive title lines, folder tabs, open-folder captions, story heading, and short body copy. This is gift-facing copy, not documentation.

output_fields.archive.titleLines: up to three short archive header lines
output_fields.tabs: row1-row4 tab title and subtitle pairs
output_fields.openedFolder.title: small opened-folder title
output_fields.openedFolder.storyBodyHtml: short rich story body; may use one mark tag for the key quoted detail

slots.language_rule: Use the user's habitual language, writing system, nickname, and warmth level for final gift text.
specificity_rules: Every line should include or clearly echo at least one concrete detail from the recipient material.
Avoid broad labels such as 'our memories', 'sweet moments', or 'forever' unless the user actually uses those words.

slots.purpose: Optional precise runtime overrides for thumbnail folders, opened-folder spread, closed preview, text replacements, and image replacements.
slots.schema_note: Maps into window.FOLDER_GIFT_CONFIG. Use imageReplacements with CSS selectors for photos/papers/stickers, textReplacements for one-off copy, and layout config objects for positions.
slots.bounds_rule: For each custom visual layer, verify the full rotated bounding box stays within the folder shell or open spread unless it is explicitly an outside cover label.

build_contract.input: filled-slots.json containing folder copy, selected/generated photos, selector image replacements, and optional layout overrides
build_contract.output: single index.html in the gift folder
build_contract.source_policy: template-source/520-folder-gift.html is canonical and read-only; build.py writes a separate output file.

activation_disclosure.message: This template downloads a large folder/paper/sticker asset bundle the first time. For the strongest version, it also benefits from identity-preserving image generation for the photos inside each folder.
activation_disclosure.show_before: fetching asset bundle or generating custom photos
cost_notes: Asset bundle download is about 120MB on first use.
Full personalization may use several AI image generations for archive photos and small scene photos.
The final gift text should be written in the user's habitual language with the recipient.

options.label: full folder-archive version
options.description: Download the asset bundle, generate folder-fitted photos, pick layered paper/tape/sticker assets, and build the complete interactive H5.

options.label: asset-only draft
options.description: Use bundled example photos and papers to make a quick draft before custom generation.

options.label: text/image fallback
options.description: Skip the heavy H5 and make a sendable image or text gift from the same recipient brief.
